item.title: Bank of Japan Holds Key Rate as Middle East War Dims Outlook
item.description: The Bank of Japan left its benchmark interest rate unchanged as uncertainty over the war in Iran and the resulting spike in energy prices clouded the economic outlook.

item.title: US-Sanctioned Chinese Refiner Hengli Restructures Singapore Unit
item.description: China’s Hengli Group Co. has changed the ownership structure of its Singapore-based oil trading arm after the US sanctioned its refining unit, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Retail Investors Return as India’s Small-, Mid-Cap Stock Rally Builds
item.description: They have bought more than $1 billion worth of shares so far this month.

item.title: South Korean Lawmakers Condemn US Pressure Over Coupang Probe
item.description: South Korean lawmakers protested what they described as US political pressure over a legal case involving Coupang Inc., with about 90 legislators planning to deliver a formal complaint to the US ambassador.

item.title: BlackRock Says Higher Government Bond Yields Are Here to Stay
item.description: Government bond yields are set to stay higher for longer as the Iran war keeps inflation elevated, according to BlackRock Investment Institute.

item.title: China Venture Capital Boosting Parallel Funds for US Investors
item.description: Chinese early stage funds are increasingly offering a fundraising structure that appeals to US investors wary of American compliance restrictions but keen to grow exposure to non-sensitive sectors in China.

item.title: OpenAI Misses Its Own User and Sales Goals, WSJ Reports
item.description: OpenAI recently failed to meet its own goals for new user acquisition and sales, fueling internal concerns that the company may struggle to support its astronomical spending on AI infrastructure, the Wall Street Journal reported.

item.title: Samsung SDI Posts Smaller-Than-Expected Loss, Sending Shares Up
item.description: South Korean battery maker Samsung SDI Co. reported a smaller-than-expected first-quarter loss, helping its shares extend a blistering rally, as demand for energy storage systems and electric cars in Europe helped cushion the impact of dwindling EV support in the US.

item.title: Japan is Ready to Act on FX at Anytime, Katayama Says Before BOJ
item.description: Japan’s authorities stand ready to respond as needed to moves in the currency market around the clock, the finance minister said, in a warning to speculators just hours before the Bank of Japan’s policy decision.

item.title: Korea Passes UK to Become World’s Eighth-Largest Stock Market
item.description: South Korea has leapfrogged the UK to become the world’s eighth-biggest stock market, fueled by a high-octane rally in its artificial intelligence-linked technology champions.

item.title: First LNG Shipment Since Iran War Began Appears to Exit Hormuz
item.description: The first liquefied natural gas shipment since the war in the Middle East began two months ago appears to have traversed the Strait of Hormuz to exit the Persian Gulf.

item.title: Advantest Shares Decline After Outlook Misses on Tight Capacity
item.description: Advantest Corp.’s stock dropped as much as 6.9% after the maker of AI chip testers gave a lackluster outlook and pointed to continued capacity constraints.

item.title: Why Anxieties Are Rising Over the Malacca Strait, Asia’s Key Shipping Route
item.description: Disruptions to shipping in the Strait of Hormuz have raised concerns about vulnerabilities in another critical maritime corridor half a world away. The Strait of Malacca — a narrow stretch of water between Indonesia and Malaysia that funnels trade past Singapore — carries more than a fifth of global maritime trade and is the world’s busiest chokepoint.

item.title: Musk v. Altman Jurors ‘Rose Up to the Plate,’ Judge Seats Nine
item.description: Nine jurors were seated Monday in the AI industry’s closely watched trial between Elon Musk and OpenAI chief Sam Altman from a pool of San Francisco Bay Area residents who are expected to hear three weeks of testimony in federal court.

item.title: Nissan Shares Climb After Forecasting Profit Instead of Loss
item.description: Nissan Motor Co. shares climbed the most in more than two months after the Japanese carmaker raised its earnings outlook, avoiding what could have been its first annual operating loss in five years.

item.title: Dynatrace Rallies on Report That Starboard Value Took a Stake
item.description: Dynatrace Inc. shares rose more than 6% in late trading on a report that Starboard Value LP took a stake and is pushing the company to better capitalize on the shift to artificial intelligence.

item.title: BYD, Geely Results to Provide Pointers in China EV Stock Rivalry
item.description: Chinese EV stocks face a key test in upcoming results, which will offer clues for investors betting on the recently diverging performance of major players.

item.title: CATL Prices $5 Billion HK Share Placement at Low End of Range
item.description: Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Ltd. has priced its $5 billion Hong Kong share placement at the bottom of the marketed range, according to terms of the deal seen by Bloomberg News.

item.title: UK Retailers Offer Bigger Discounts as Shopper Confidence Wanes
item.description: UK retailers are discounting heavily to lure shoppers amid weakening consumer confidence, underscoring the risks facing the sector as it braces for higher costs linked to the conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: Rising Yield Outlook Makes Japan’s Life Insurers Go Slow on JGBs
item.description: The likelihood of further interest rate hikes in Japan is keeping many of its life insurers from actively purchasing the nation’s government bonds this year.

item.title: Pre-War Playbook Returns as Stock Traders Favor Asia Over US
item.description: Investors are reverting to a pre-war playbook of betting Asian stocks will outpace US peers, as confidence builds in the region’s central role in the artificial-intelligence boom.

item.title: Optical Computing Firm Lightelligence Set for HK Debut After IPO
item.description: Lightelligence, a Chinese optical-computing provider, is set to begin trading in Hong Kong on Tuesday after raising HK$2.5 billion ($323 million) in an initial public offering, further fueling investor interest in the fast-surging industry that’s a key provider of parts used in the artificial-intelligence buildout.

item.title: Gold Steadies as Traders Weigh Diplomatic Push to End Iran War
item.description: Gold was little changed as traders weighed the latest efforts by the US and Iran to find a negotiated solution to the two-month war that’s choked energy supplies and heightened inflation risks.

item.title: Some US Tariff-Relief Deals Are Worthless, Canada’s Carney Tells CBC
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney told the Canadian Broadcasting Corp. he’s in no hurry to sign a minor deal for tariff relief with the US, arguing that other governments that have done so aren’t happy with the results.

item.title: Oil Steadies as US Weighs Iran Proposal With Hormuz Still Shut
item.description: Oil held a gain as traders weighed the next steps toward peace talks over the Iran war, with the US discussing a proposal from Tehran while the crucial Strait of Hormuz remained almost impassable.

item.title: Asian Stocks Look Subdued Ahead of Tech Earnings: Markets Wrap
item.description: Asian stocks were poised for a soft open Tuesday as investors braced for a wave of earnings from tech giants while keeping a close eye on developments in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Vietnam Gas Major Looks to US as Iran War Reorders LPG Flows
item.description: PetroVietnam Gas JSC plans to import more liquefied petroleum gas from the US than its traditional Middle East suppliers next month, in the latest sign that the Iran war is reordering global energy flows.

item.title: Mexico Arrests Cartel Boss With $5 Million US Bounty on His Head
item.description: Mexico arrested a senior underworld figure with a $5 million bounty on his head who was sought for crimes including drug trafficking and defrauding US pensioners.

item.title: How Asia-Pacific Is Fighting a Fuel Shock That Could Get Worse
item.description: Asian nations face the prospect of prolonged strain on crucial energy supplies as the conflict in the Middle East grinds past the two-month mark, with the Strait of Hormuz still largely off-limits to shipping.

item.title: Match Invests $100 Million in Grindr Rival Sniffies With Option to Buy
item.description: Match Group Inc., whose dating-app portfolio includes brands like Hinge, Tinder and Match.com, is investing $100 million in Sniffies, a platform for queer men, paving the way for a possible acquisition.
